text: Temperature Scaling in Softmax. Controlling probability distribution sharpness.
The softmax function converts logits into probabilities using the formula: P of i equals e to the power of z sub i over T, divided by the sum of all e to the power of z sub j over T.
Starting with temperature equals one, representing standard softmax behavior.
Low temperature, point three, produces a sharp peaked distribution. The model becomes highly confident in one class.
Temperature point eight creates a somewhat sharper distribution, still favoring high logits but less extreme.
Temperature two point zero flattens the distribution. Probabilities spread more evenly across classes.
Very high temperature, five point zero, creates a nearly uniform distribution. The model becomes uncertain and outputs similar probabilities for all classes.
Returning to temperature equals one, the balanced setting.

Summary: Low temperature produces confident predictions with sharp peaks, while high temperature creates uncertain predictions with flattened distributions. Temperature scaling lets us control model confidence.
